_meta.description: Active credit card transfer bonuses. Each bonus shows the current promotional ratio along with primary sources verifying it. Refreshed weekly via scripts/refresh-transfer-bonuses.py which scrapes Frequent Miler and cross-checks against AwardWallet. Confidence levels per Research Integrity Protocol: VERIFIED = both FM and AwardWallet agree; LIKELY = only FM; UNVERIFIED = inferred or single mention without strong source.
_meta.manual_consult_note: TPG is referenced only when manually investigating a discrepancy. It is NOT part of the automated refresh because TPG frequently shows expired bonuses as active.

schema_notes.ratio: Multiplier on the standard transfer ratio. 1.20 = 20% bonus = 1000 points becomes 1200 miles (when standard ratio is 1:1).
schema_notes.effective_ratio: Final transfer ratio after the bonus. E.g., Capital One -> JAL standard is 4:3 (0.75); 30% bonus makes it 0.975.
schema_notes.end_date_inclusive: ISO date. Bonus is active through end of this date in program's home timezone.
schema_notes.confidence: VERIFIED, LIKELY, or UNVERIFIED per Research Integrity Protocol
schema_notes.targeted: true if offer is targeted (not available to all cardholders); false or omitted if public

active_bonuses.transfer_example: 1,000 UR points -> 1,200 Aeroplan points (or 1,300 for Aeroplan Visa cardholders, which adds a stackable 10% cardholder bonus)
active_bonuses.notes: Aeroplan is often the best Star Alliance booking program (no fuel surcharges on most partners, generous routing rules, mixed cabins). Aeroplan Visa cardholders get an additional 10% transfer bonus that stacks for an effective 30% bonus.

Frequent Miler title says 'but you shouldn't' - IHG points are valued ~0.5 cpp while Chase UR is ~2 cpp via portal or 2+ cpp via airline transfers. Even with 70% bonus, the math is roughly 1 UR -> 1.7 IHG = ~0.85 cpp value. Skip unless you have a specific high-end IHG redemption you've already priced and confirmed.

active_bonuses.transfer_example: 1,000 Capital One miles -> 975 JAL miles (standard ratio is 4:3 = 0.75; 30% bonus brings it close to but not quite 1:1)
active_bonuses.notes: JAL Mileage Bank is a oneworld program. Strong sweet spots: JAL -> Asia is well-priced, especially in business and first class. Capital One's standard 4:3 ratio to JAL is mediocre; this 30% bonus brings it close to 1:1 effective.

active_bonuses.transfer_example: 1,000 Citi TYP -> 1,250 LHW Leaders Club points (when transferred via the eligible Citi card combo)
active_bonuses.notes: Confidence is LIKELY rather than VERIFIED because only Frequent Miler currently mentions this bonus - TPG and AwardWallet have not yet picked it up. LHW is a niche luxury hotel collection. Useful primarily if you have a specific upcoming LHW stay; otherwise skip.
Hilton transfers from MR are usually a poor use of MR (TPG values MR at ~2 cpp, Hilton at ~0.5 cpp, so 1:2.4 = ~1.2 cpp effective). Only consider for a specific high-value Hilton redemption.

expired_recently.note: TPG's transfer-bonuses page (last updated April 1) still listed this as active when checked on Apr 29, but Frequent Miler shows it expired April 8. TPG content is stale.
Recently expired. Avianca LifeMiles bonuses recur frequently (see history).
Recently expired. Virgin Atlantic bonuses recur frequently.

frequent_recurring_bonuses_to_watch._note: These bonuses tend to repeat several times per year based on FM's historical data. If your travel timeline is flexible, waiting for one of these to recur is often worthwhile.

amex_mr_to_avios.to_display: British Airways / Iberia / Aer Lingus Avios
amex_mr_to_avios.history_note: FM logs show this in 2024-08, 2024-10, 2025-06, 2025-07. Predictable cadence.

chase_ur_to_aeroplan.history_note: FM logs show this in 2024-12, 2025-06 (+10% cardholder), 2025-11 (up to 25%), 2026-04. Stackable with Aeroplan Visa cardholder bonus.

chase_ur_to_marriott.history_note: FM logs show this in 2024-09 through 2025-02, 2025-06, 2025-11, 2026-02. Marriott points are valued low (~0.7 cpp) so bonus rarely changes the math meaningfully.

chase_ur_to_virgin_atlantic.history_note: Strong Virgin Atlantic bonuses appear regularly. Combined with Virgin's ANA First Class sweet spot, these are high-value windows.

capital_one_to_avianca.history_note: Avianca LifeMiles is one of the better Star Alliance booking programs.

bilt_rent_day_monthly.frequency: Monthly on Rent Day (1st of month)
bilt_rent_day_monthly.history_note: Bilt's Rent Day bonus structure: a high bonus to one or two transfer partners on the 1st of each month, lasting 24 hours. Past partners include Avios, JAL, Avianca, Hilton, Wyndham, Accor, Etihad, Virgin Atlantic. Highly recommended to plan transfers for Rent Day.

decision_rules.rule_1_specific_redemption: Only transfer points when you have a specific redemption priced and confirmed. Transfers are irreversible.
decision_rules.rule_2_factor_in_bonus: When evaluating a bonus, compute the effective ratio (standard_ratio * bonus_multiplier) and the resulting cpp. Compare cpp to the floor cpp from points-valuations skill.
decision_rules.rule_3_ihg_marriott_skepticism: Hotel program bonuses (especially IHG and Marriott from Chase UR) often look attractive but typically don't beat the actual Chase portal Points Boost quote even at 70%+ bonuses. Pull the live portal quote and run the math against it, not against an assumed flat rate.
decision_rules.rule_4_stackable_bonuses: Some bonuses stack with cardholder-specific bonuses (e.g., Chase UR to Aeroplan + Aeroplan Visa cardholder bonus = effective 30%+ bonus). Always check if you hold the relevant card.
decision_rules.rule_5_targeted_offers: Offers marked 'targeted' may not be available to all cardholders. Check your account before counting on them.
decision_rules.rule_6_check_for_recent_expiry: If a bonus appeared on TPG but doesn't appear on Frequent Miler current bonuses, it likely just expired. Frequent Miler's table is the canonical realtime source.
